subject: Product Management
title: Netflix's Artwork Tests
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Netflix's Artwork Tests.

hook.text: Netflix discovered that artwork — the thumbnail image — is the single biggest factor in whether users click on a show.

buildup.text: Netflix tests dozens of artwork variants for every title.

discovery.text: They found that showing recognizable faces increases clicks. Expressive emotions outperform neutral ones.

twist.text: Netflix personalizes artwork per user. One person sees a romance scene for a movie while another sees the action scene.

climax.text: Personalized artwork increased engagement significantly. The experiment proved that context — not just content.

punchline.text: The same product, framed differently, appeals to different people. Context is everything.

quiz.question: What did Netflix's artwork experiments reveal?
options: Thumbnail artwork is the biggest factor in clicks, and personalizing it per user significantly increases engagement
All users prefer the same artwork style
Artwork has no measurable impact on viewing choices
